title: Astracantha dolona
intro: Astracantha dolona, also known as Astragalus dolon, is a type of perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha dolona include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Astracantha dolona include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Astracantha dolona is classified as a species of thorny shrub or small tree in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Astracantha. The genus Astracantha is a group of thorny plants found mainly in the Mediterranean region. Astracantha dolona is native to parts of the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The classification of Astracantha dolona is further detailed by its binomial nomenclature, but the exact taxonomic classification can be complex and subject to ongoing research.
